selected_problem_solved:
The proof establishes that for
\[
w=a\,b\,B\,A\,c\,C\,a\,d\,D\,A\,e\,E\,a\,f\,F\,A
\]
the word is reducible and quasi-reduced; every admissible matching has the five forced uncrossed caps \((2,3),(5,6),(8,9),(11,12),(14,15)\); the remaining six-point skeleton has at most two crossings; and the three displayed two-cells \(Q_1,Q_2,Q_3\) form a nonzero mod-\(2\) cellular \(2\)-cycle. Since there are no \(3\)-cells, this gives \(H_2(F_w;\mathbb F_2)\neq 0\). Therefore this \(F_w\) is not contractible, so the proposed general contractibility statement is false.
selector_skipped_reason: N_PARALLEL_RUNS=1 — single run, no selection needed

all_runs.problem_solved:
The proof establishes that for
\[
w=a\,b\,B\,A\,c\,C\,a\,d\,D\,A\,e\,E\,a\,f\,F\,A
\]
the word is reducible and quasi-reduced; every admissible matching has the five forced uncrossed caps \((2,3),(5,6),(8,9),(11,12),(14,15)\); the remaining six-point skeleton has at most two crossings; and the three displayed two-cells \(Q_1,Q_2,Q_3\) form a nonzero mod-\(2\) cellular \(2\)-cycle. Since there are no \(3\)-cells, this gives \(H_2(F_w;\mathbb F_2)\neq 0\). Therefore this \(F_w\) is not contractible, so the proposed general contractibility statement is false.
